document_title: Diploma in Teacher Education (DTE) - Microteaching Guide

sub_strands.name: 1.1 The Concept of Microteaching
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the meaning of microteaching
Assess the importance of microteaching in teacher training
Analyse the characteristics of microteaching
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees discuss the meaning and importance of microteaching.
Teacher trainees watch video demonstrations of microteaching sessions.
Teacher trainees brainstorm on the characteristics of microteaching.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to explain the meaning of microteaching

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively and clearly explains the meaning of microteaching
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly explains the meaning of microteaching
levels.Approaches Expectation: To a certain extent, clearly explains the meaning of microteaching
levels.Below Expectation: Only with prompts, explains the meaning of microteaching

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to assess the importance of microteaching in teacher training

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Extensively and logically assesses the importance of microteaching in teacher training
levels.Meets Expectation: Assesses the importance of microteaching in teacher training
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially assesses the importance of microteaching in teacher training
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, assesses the importance of microteaching in teacher training

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse the characteristics of microteaching

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively analyses the characteristics of microteaching
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses the characteristics of microteaching
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses the characteristics of microteaching
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, analyses the characteristics of microteaching

sub_strands.name: 1.2 Phases in Microteaching
specific_learning_outcomes: Assess the phases in microteaching
Demonstrate understanding of the cyclical nature of microteaching
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees discuss the various phases of microteaching: planning, teaching, observation, feedback, re-planning, re-teaching, and reflection.
Teacher trainees view recorded microteaching sessions and identify the different phases.
In groups, trainees map out a microteaching cycle based on a case study.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to assess the phases in microteaching

levels.Exceeds Expectation: With examples, correctly assesses the phases in microteaching
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly assesses the phases in microteaching
levels.Approaches Expectation: To a certain extent, correctly assesses the phases in microteaching
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, assesses the phases in microteaching

sub_strands.name: 2.4 Strand and Sub strand
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse the strands and sub strands in a specific subject in the curriculum
Relate concepts in the sub strand to the strand in the curriculum design
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees make presentations on the ideas addressed in each strand within a given subject.
Teacher trainees brainstorm on the link between concepts in the sub strands and their respective strands.
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: What is the difference between a strand and sub strand?
How do concepts in sub strands relate to the strands?

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse the strands and sub strands in a specific subject in the curriculum

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Elaborately and accurately analyses the strands and sub strands in a specific subject in the curriculum
levels.Meets Expectation: Accurately analyses the strands and sub strands in a specific subject in the curriculum
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses the strands and sub strands in a specific subject in the curriculum
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, analyses the strands and sub strands in a specific subject in the curriculum

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to relate concepts in the sub strand to the strand in the curriculum design

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Logically and correctly relates concepts in the sub strand to the strand in the curriculum design
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly relates concepts in the sub strand to the strand in the curriculum design
levels.Approaches Expectation: Relates some concepts in the sub strand to the strand in the curriculum design
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, relates concepts in the sub strand to the strand in the curriculum design

sub_strands.name: 2.5 Specific Learning Outcomes
specific_learning_outcomes: Interpret specific learning outcomes in a sub strand
Formulate lesson-specific learning outcomes from the sub strand’s specific learning outcomes
Acknowledge the implication of learning outcomes in the learning process
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees interpret sample specific learning outcomes from various subjects.
Trainees develop lesson-specific learning outcomes based on curriculum documents.
Group discussion on how learning outcomes guide teaching and assessment.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to interpret specific learning outcomes in a sub strand

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Elaborately interprets specific learning outcomes in a sub strand
levels.Meets Expectation: Interprets specific learning outcomes in a sub strand
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially interprets specific learning outcomes in a sub strand
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, interprets specific learning outcomes in a sub strand

sub_strands.name: 2.6 Suggested Key Inquiry Questions
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine the characteristics of good suggested key inquiry questions
Suggest key inquiry questions that will guide the facilitation of a lesson
Demonstrate the use of suggested key inquiry questions in a lesson
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees analyse sample inquiry questions from curriculum materials.
In pairs, trainees formulate inquiry questions for given topics.
Trainees role-play lesson introductions using inquiry questions.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine the characteristics of good suggested key inquiry questions

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Broadly and accurately examines the characteristics of good suggested key inquiry questions
levels.Meets Expectation: Accurately examines the characteristics of good suggested key inquiry questions
levels.Approaches Expectation: To some degree, accurately examines the characteristics of good suggested key inquiry questions
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, examines characteristics of good suggested key inquiry questions

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to suggest key inquiry questions that will guide the facilitation of a lesson

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Illustratively suggests key inquiry questions that will guide the facilitation of a lesson
levels.Meets Expectation: Suggests key inquiry questions that will guide the facilitation of a lesson
levels.Approaches Expectation: Suggests some key inquiry questions that will guide the facilitation of a lesson
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, suggests key inquiry questions that will guide the facilitation of a lesson

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to demonstrate the use of suggested key inquiry questions in a lesson

levels.Exceeds Expectation: With examples, clearly demonstrates the use of suggested key inquiry questions in a lesson
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly demonstrates the use of suggested key inquiry questions in a lesson
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially demonstrates the use of suggested key inquiry questions in a lesson
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, demonstrates the use of suggested key inquiry questions in a lesson

sub_strands.name: 2.7 Core Competencies
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse the core competencies outlined in the BECF
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees study the Basic Education Curriculum Framework (BECF) document.
Group discussions on the six core competencies: communication, critical thinking, digital literacy, learning to learn, self-efficacy, and values.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse the core competencies outlined in the BECF

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively and clearly analyses the core competencies outlined in the BECF
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly analyses the core competencies outlined in the BECF
levels.Approaches Expectation: Clearly analyses some core competencies outlined in the BECF
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, analyses core competencies outlined in the BECF

sub_strands.name: 2.8 Values
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify the core values to be developed by learners as per BECF
Discuss learning experiences that promote the acquisition of values among learners
Organize and facilitate micro lessons to demonstrate facilitation of learning experiences to promote the acquisition of values
suggested_learning_experiences: Search for information on the internet and other sources on the meaning of values
Refer to the BECF to identify the core values: patriotism, nationalism, respect, peace, unity, love
Discuss the learning experiences that promote the acquisition of values among learners
Organize and facilitate micro lessons to demonstrate facilitation of learning experiences to promote the acquisition of values (formal, informal, non-formal)
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: How does a teacher promote acquisition of values in the learning process?

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to illustrate experiences that lead to the acquisition of values by learners

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Precisely and correctly illustrates experiences that lead to the acquisition of values by learners
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly illustrates experiences that lead to the acquisition of values by learners
levels.Approaches Expectation: Correctly illustrates some experiences that lead to the acquisition of values by learners
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, illustrates experiences that lead to the acquisition of values by learners

sub_strands.name: 3.1 Nature of Professional Documents
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine the use of professional documents in curriculum implementation and assessment
Assess how professional documents make teaching and learning more effective
Interpret the different types of professional documents used by a school teacher
Appreciate the use of professional documents for a teacher
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees critique the function of professional documents.
Teacher trainees search information on the internet on the importance of professional documents.
Teacher trainees discuss and write how professional documents promote teaching and learning.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine the use of professional documents in curriculum implementation and assessment

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Extensively examines the use of professional documents in curriculum implementation and assessment
levels.Meets Expectation: Examines the use of professional documents in curriculum implementation and assessment
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partly examines the use of professional documents in curriculum implementation and assessment
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, examines the use of professional documents in curriculum implementation and assessment

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to assess how professional documents make teaching and learning more effective

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Elaborately assesses how professional documents make teaching and learning more effective
levels.Meets Expectation: Assesses how professional documents make teaching and learning more effective
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially assesses how professional documents make teaching and learning more effective
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, assesses how professional documents make teaching and learning more effective

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to interpret the different types of professional documents used by a school teacher

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively interprets the different types of professional documents used by a school teacher
levels.Meets Expectation: Interprets the different types of professional documents used by a school teacher
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially interprets the different types of professional documents used by a school teacher
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, interprets the different types of professional documents used by a school teacher

sub_strands.name: 3.2 Records of Work
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse the use of records of work to evaluate performance over time in relation to the scheme of work
Prepare relevant records of work for use in the teaching and learning process
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees generate samples of records of work for different subjects.
Use ICT tools to create digital records of work.
Discuss and present on the use of records of work in monitoring learner progress.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse the use of records of work to evaluate performance over a period of time in relation to the scheme of work

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively analyses the use of records of work to evaluate performance over a period of time in relation to the scheme of work
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses the use of records of work to evaluate performance over a period of time in relation to the scheme of work
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses the use of records of work to evaluate performance over a period of time in relation to the scheme of work
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, analyses the use of records of work to evaluate performance over a period of time in relation to the scheme of work

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to prepare relevant records of work for use in the teaching and learning process

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively and accurately prepares relevant records of work for use in the teaching and learning process
levels.Meets Expectation: Accurately prepares relevant records of work for use in the teaching and learning process
levels.Approaches Expectation: Prepares relevant records of work for use in the teaching and learning process with minor errors
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, prepares relevant records of work for use in the teaching and learning process

sub_strands.name: 3.3 Lesson Plan
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine the components and administrative details of a lesson plan
Illustrate the relevance of a lesson plan to a teacher and the learning process
Develop a lesson plan using the provided format
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse sample lesson plans from experienced teachers.
Identify key components such as objectives, resources, activities, differentiation, assessment.
Create original lesson plans based on curriculum topics.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine the components and administrative details of a lesson plan

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Broadly examines the components and administrative details of a lesson plan
levels.Meets Expectation: Examines the components and administrative details of a lesson plan
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partly examines the components and administrative details of a lesson plan
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance examines the components and administrative details of a lesson plan

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to illustrate the relevance of a lesson plan to a teacher and the learning process

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively illustrates the relevance of a lesson plan to a teacher and the learning process
levels.Meets Expectation: Illustrates the relevance of a lesson plan to a teacher and the learning process
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partly illustrates the relevance of a lesson plan to a teacher and the learning process
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, illustrates the relevance of a lesson plan to a teacher and the learning process

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to develop a lesson plan using the provided format

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively and accurately develops a comprehensive lesson plan using the provided format
levels.Meets Expectation: Accurately develops a lesson plan using the provided format
levels.Approaches Expectation: Develops a lesson plan using the provided format with minor omissions
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, develops a lesson plan using the provided format

sub_strands.name: 3.4 Scheme of Work
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse the components of a scheme of work
Develop a scheme of work using the provided format
suggested_learning_experiences: Review sample schemes of work for various subjects and terms.
Break down a term's curriculum into weekly topics and allocations.
Create a scheme of work for a chosen subject and term.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse the components of a scheme of work

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively analyses the components of a scheme of work
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses the components of a scheme of work
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses the components of a scheme of work
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, analyses the components of a scheme of work

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to develop a scheme of work using the provided format

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively and accurately develops a comprehensive scheme of work using the provided format
levels.Meets Expectation: Accurately develops a scheme of work using the provided format
levels.Approaches Expectation: Develops a scheme of work using the provided format with minor errors
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, develops a scheme of work using the provided format

sub_strands.name: 3.5 Individualized Education Programme (IEP)
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the concept of an Individualized Education Programme (IEP)
Analyse the components of an IEP
Develop an IEP for a learner with special needs
suggested_learning_experiences: Study case studies of learners with diverse needs.
Research the purpose and structure of IEPs.
Draft an IEP for a hypothetical or real learner based on assessment data.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to explain the concept of an Individualized Education Programme (IEP)

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively and clearly explains the concept of an IEP
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly explains the concept of an IEP
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially explains the concept of an IEP
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, explains the concept of an IEP

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse the components of an IEP

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Thoroughly analyses all components of an IEP
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses the components of an IEP
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses the components of an IEP
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, analyses the components of an IEP

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to develop an IEP for a learner with special needs

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively and comprehensively develops a realistic and effective IEP for a learner with special needs
levels.Meets Expectation: Develops a valid IEP for a learner with special needs
levels.Approaches Expectation: Develops an IEP for a learner with special needs with significant guidance
levels.Below Expectation: Only with extensive assistance, develops an IEP for a learner with special needs

sub_strands.name: 4.1 Concept of Pedagogy
specific_learning_outcomes: Evaluate the concept of pedagogy and pedagogical approaches in the learning process
suggested_learning_experiences: Define pedagogy and distinguish it from related concepts like andragogy.
Discuss historical and contemporary pedagogical theories.
Debate the relevance of various pedagogical approaches in modern classrooms.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to evaluate the concept of pedagogy and pedagogical approaches in the learning process

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Exhaustively evaluates the concept of pedagogy and pedagogical approaches in the learning process
levels.Meets Expectation: Evaluates the concept of pedagogy and pedagogical approaches in the learning process
levels.Approaches Expectation: Attempts to evaluate the concept of pedagogy and pedagogical approaches in the learning process
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support evaluates the concept of pedagogy and pedagogical approaches in the learning process

sub_strands.name: 4.2 Experiential Learning Approach
specific_learning_outcomes: Evaluate experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
Apply experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Engage in hands-on activities simulating experiential learning (e.g., science experiments, field simulations).
Analyse Kolb’s experiential learning cycle.
Design and deliver a short lesson using the experiential learning model.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to evaluate experiential learning approach in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Critically evaluates experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Evaluates experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Evaluates some aspects of experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, evaluates experiential learning approach in lesson delivery

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply experiential learning approach in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively applies experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially applies experiential learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, applies experiential learning approach in lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 4.3 Inquiry-Based Learning Approach
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine inquiry based learning approach
Apply inquiry based learning approach in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse examples of inquiry-based lessons.
Design a lesson using the inquiry-based model (posing questions, investigation, conclusion).
Deliver a micro lesson demonstrating inquiry-based techniques.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine inquiry based learning approach

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively and correctly examines inquiry based learning approach
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly examines inquiry based learning approach
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partly and correctly examines inquiry based learning approach
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, examines inquiry based learning approach

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply inquiry based learning approach in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively applies inquiry based learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies inquiry based learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially applies inquiry based learning approach in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, applies inquiry based learning approach in lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 4.4 Problem-Based Learning Approach
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine problem-based approach in teaching and learning
Apply problem-based approach in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse real-world problems suitable for classroom use.
Design a problem-based learning scenario.
Facilitate a micro lesson using the problem-based approach.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine problem-based approach in teaching and learning

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Critically and clearly examines problem-based approach in teaching and learning
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly examines problem-based approach in teaching and learning
levels.Approaches Expectation: To some extent, clearly examines problem-based approach in teaching and learning
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, examines problem-based approach in teaching and learning

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply problem-based approach in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively and correctly applies problem-based approach in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly applies problem-based approach in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Correctly applies some aspects of problem-based approach in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, applies problem-based approach in lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 4.5 Project-Based Learning Approach
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine project-based approach in the learning process
Apply project-based approach in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Study successful project-based learning examples.
Plan a cross-curricular project for learners.
Simulate the launch of a project-based activity during a micro lesson.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine project-based approach in the learning process

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Exhaustively and clearly examines project-based approach in the learning process
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly examines project-based approach in the learning process
levels.Approaches Expectation: Clearly examines some aspects of project-based approach in the learning process
levels.Below Expectation: Only with prompts examines project-based approach in the learning process

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply project-based approach in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively applies project-based approach in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies project-based approach in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially applies project-based approach in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, applies project-based approach in lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 4.6 Facilitation Approach
specific_learning_outcomes: Illustrate the pros and cons of facilitation approach in lesson delivery
Apply facilitation approach in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Compare teacher-centered vs. learner-centered models.
Observe videos of facilitative teaching.
Conduct a learner-centered discussion session during microteaching.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to illustrate the pros and cons of facilitation approach in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Elaborately and correctly illustrates the pros and cons of facilitation approach in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly illustrates the pros and cons of facilitation approach in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: To some extent, correctly illustrates the pros and cons of facilitation approach in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, illustrates the pros and cons of facilitation approach in lesson delivery

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply facilitation approach in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively applies facilitation approach in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies facilitation approach in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially applies facilitation approach in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, applies facilitation approach in lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 4.7 Reflective Learning Approach
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse aspects of reflective learning as used in the learning process
Illustrate the pros and cons of reflective learning in a lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Engage in journaling after teaching practice sessions.
Read and discuss Schön’s theory of reflection-in-action and reflection-on-action.
Design activities that promote learner reflection (e.g., exit tickets, portfolios).
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse aspects of reflective learning as used in the learning process

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Exhaustively analyses aspects of reflective learning as used in the learning process
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses aspects of reflective learning as used in the learning process
levels.Approaches Expectation: Analyses some aspects of reflective learning as used in the learning process
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance analyses aspects of reflective learning as used in the learning process

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to illustrate the pros and cons of reflective learning in a lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Elaborately and correctly illustrates the pros and cons of reflective learning in a lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly illustrates the pros and cons of reflective learning in a lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Correctly illustrates some of the pros and cons of reflective learning in a lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance illustrates the pros and cons of reflective learning in a lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 4.8 Collaborative and Cooperative Learning
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the concept of collaborative and cooperative learning
Apply cooperative and collaborative learning approaches to deliver a lesson
suggested_learning_experiences: Participate in group tasks using cooperative learning structures (e.g., jigsaw, think-pair-share).
Differentiate between collaboration and cooperation.
Deliver a micro lesson incorporating group work strategies.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to explain the concept of collaborative and cooperative learning

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively and clearly explains the concept of collaborative and cooperative learning
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly explains the concept of collaborative and cooperative learning
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially explains the concept of collaborative and cooperative learning
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, explains the concept of collaborative and cooperative learning

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply cooperative and collaborative learning approaches to deliver a lesson

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Clearly applies cooperative and collaborative learning approaches to deliver a lesson
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies cooperative and collaborative learning approaches to deliver a lesson
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially applies cooperative and collaborative learning approaches to deliver a lesson to some extent
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, applies cooperative and collaborative learning approaches to deliver a lesson

sub_strands.name: 5.1 Questioning Techniques
specific_learning_outcomes: Explore effective questioning techniques in lesson facilitation
Demonstrate effective use of questioning techniques in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse Bloom’s taxonomy and corresponding question stems.
Practice formulating open-ended, probing, and higher-order questions.
Deliver a micro lesson focusing on varied questioning techniques.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to explore effective questioning techniques in lesson facilitation

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Broadly explores effective questioning techniques in lesson facilitation
levels.Meets Expectation: Explores effective questioning techniques in lesson facilitation
levels.Approaches Expectation: Explores some effective questioning techniques in lesson facilitation.
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, explores effective questioning techniques in lesson facilitation.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to demonstrate effective use of questioning techniques in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Consistently and appropriately uses a variety of effective questioning techniques in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Uses effective questioning techniques in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Uses some effective questioning techniques in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with prompting, uses questioning techniques in lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 5.2 Stimulus Variation
specific_learning_outcomes: Evaluate techniques applied to enhance stimulus variation in a lesson
Demonstrate a lesson to peers using stimulus variation techniques
suggested_learning_experiences: Watch video clips highlighting voice modulation, movement, and gaze.
Practice changing tone, pitch, and pace of speech.
Deliver a micro lesson focusing on physical movement and visual aids.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to evaluate techniques applied to enhance stimulus variation in a lesson

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Critically evaluates techniques applied to enhance stimulus variation in a lesson
levels.Meets Expectation: Evaluates techniques applied to enhance stimulus variation in a lesson
levels.Approaches Expectation: Logically evaluates some techniques applied to enhance stimulus variation in a lesson
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, evaluates techniques applied to enhance stimulus variation in a lesson

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to demonstrate a lesson to peers using stimulus variation techniques

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively and skillfully demonstrates a lesson to peers using stimulus variation techniques
levels.Meets Expectation: Skillfully demonstrates a lesson to peers using stimulus variation techniques
levels.Approaches Expectation: Sometimes, skillfully demonstrates a lesson to peers using stimulus variation techniques
levels.Below Expectation: Only with prompts, demonstrates a lesson to peers using stimulus variation techniques

sub_strands.name: 5.3 Board Work
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate proper use of a white board or chalkboard during a lesson
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice clear, organized board writing with headings, spacing, and color coding.
Peer review of board work clarity and relevance.
Deliver a micro lesson emphasizing effective board use.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to demonstrate proper use of a white board or chalkboard during a lesson

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Elaborately demonstrates proper use of a white board or chalkboard during a lesson
levels.Meets Expectation: Demonstrates proper use of a white board or chalkboard during a lesson
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially demonstrates proper use of a white board or chalkboard during a lesson
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, demonstrates proper use of a white board or chalkboard during a lesson

sub_strands.name: 5.4 Reinforcement
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse reinforcement skills in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Identify verbal, non-verbal, and symbolic forms of reinforcement.
Analyse video clips showing positive reinforcement in classrooms.
Role-play scenarios using affirmative feedback techniques.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse reinforcement skills in lesson delivery

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively analyses reinforcement skills in lesson delivery
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses reinforcement skills in lesson delivery
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses reinforcement skills in lesson delivery
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, analyses reinforcement skills in lesson delivery

sub_strands.name: 5.5 Lesson Introduction
specific_learning_outcomes: Assess the importance of executing a good lesson introduction
Execute the introduction of a lesson effectively
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse effective lesson starters (hooks, questions, visuals).
Design engaging introductions for sample topics.
Deliver a micro lesson focusing on a strong opening.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to assess the importance of executing a good lesson introduction

levels.Exceeds Expectation: With examples, assesses the importance of executing a good lesson introduction
levels.Meets Expectation: Assesses the importance of executing a good lesson introduction
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partly assesses the importance of executing a good lesson introduction
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, assesses the importance of executing a good lesson introduction

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to execute the introduction of a lesson effectively

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Expertly executes the introduction of a lesson effectively
levels.Meets Expectation: Executes the introduction of a lesson effectively
levels.Approaches Expectation: To some degree, executes the introduction of a lesson effectively
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, executes the introduction of a lesson effectively

sub_strands.name: 5.6 Lesson Conclusion
specific_learning_outcomes: Assess the importance of executing a good lesson conclusion
Execute the conclusion of a lesson effectively
suggested_learning_experiences: Study techniques for summarizing and consolidating learning.
Design closure activities (exit tickets, summaries, reflections).
Deliver a micro lesson with a deliberate focus on concluding effectively.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to assess the importance of executing a good lesson conclusion

levels.Exceeds Expectation: With examples, assesses the importance of executing a good lesson conclusion
levels.Meets Expectation: Assesses the importance of executing a good lesson conclusion
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partly assesses the importance of executing a good lesson conclusion
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, assesses the importance of executing a good lesson conclusion

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to execute the conclusion of a lesson effectively

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Expertly executes the conclusion of a lesson effectively
levels.Meets Expectation: Executes the conclusion of a lesson effectively
levels.Approaches Expectation: To some degree, executes the conclusion of a lesson effectively
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, executes the conclusion of a lesson effectively

sub_strands.name: 6.1 Planning for Microteaching
specific_learning_outcomes: Plan for a micro lesson to be delivered in 10 minutes for an effective experience
suggested_learning_experiences: Select a topic and narrow it to a 10-minute scope.
Write specific learning outcomes, select appropriate activities, and prepare necessary resources.
Practice the 10-minute lesson individually before peer teaching.
